problem text: Given the following details: $\mathbb{E}[X_1] = -3$, $\mathbb{E}[X_2] = 7$, $\text{Var}[X_1] = 24$, $\text{Var}[X_2] = 6$. Determine the greatest possible value for $\text{Cov}(X_1, X_2)$.
problem solution: The correlation coefficient $\rho$ is expressed as $\rho = \frac{\text{Cov}(X_1, X_2)}{\sigma_1 \sigma_2}, \text{ where } \sigma_1^2 = \text{Var}(X_1) \text{ and } \sigma_2^2 = \text{Var}(X_2).$ It is known that the correlation coefficient must fulfill $-1 \leq \rho \leq 1.$ $-1 \leq \frac{\text{Cov}(X_1, X_2)}{\sigma_1 \sigma_2} \leq 1$ $-1 \leq \frac{\text{Cov}(X_1, X_2)}{12} \leq 1$ $-12 \leq \text{Cov}(X_1, X_2) \leq 12$ The greatest possible value for $\text{Cov}(X_1, X_2)$ is $12$.
problem hint: Consider utilizing the definition of the correlation coefficient
